copy.keyTakeaway: Pirates beat Astros, Guardians demolish Yankees, Brewers cruise past Giants. Knicks-Spurs tip tonight. Tech stocks mixed—Microsoft down 4.2%, Apple up 2.9%. Rick Adelman, legendary Trail Blazers coach, dies at 79.

todaysHits.sports: Pirates shock Astros; Guardians rout Yankees; Brewers handle Giants.
todaysHits.golf: Brian Campbell leads Memorial Tournament after first round.
todaysHits.f1: Monaco Grand Prix this weekend—prep the schedule.
todaysHits.worldcup: World Cup 2026 kicks off June 11—eight days.
todaysHits.culture: Steph Curry signs with Li-Ning; Vanilla Ice plays anyone; Rick Adelman remembered.

lead.headline: Guardians just dismantled the Yankees at home
lead.whatHappened: Cleveland rolled into the Bronx and beat New York 9-4, making the Yankees look flat in their own stadium. The Guardians' offense got going early and never let up.
lead.whyBullet1: The Yankees have been shaky all season and this loss exposes how much they're struggling to string together wins against real teams.
lead.whyBullet2: Cleveland's in the conversation now—they showed they can go into hostile territory and dominate, which matters come October.
lead.whatToSay: Yankees fans are panic-watching right now, but honestly the Guardians just came to play and New York didn't show up.

markets.mood: Tech stocks got hammered on Microsoft's -4.2% crater, but Apple bounced +2.9% and Tesla jumped +1.9%, suggesting investors are rotating out of software and into hardware and EVs rather than hitting the eject button.
markets.whyBullet1: Your 401k's tech weighting just shifted if you hold a broad index fund—MSFT's drop hit the Magnificent Seven hard, so check whether your target-date fund is overexposed to single names.
markets.whyBullet2: Watch for earnings revisions next week as analysts digest Microsoft's miss and decide if it's a one-off or a sign the AI spending cycle is disappointing; any guidance cut from the cloud providers (Thursday-Friday) matters.
markets.bringUp: Microsoft down 4.2% while Apple climbed 2.9% on the same day tells you the market's not just selling tech—it's picking winners and losers within it, which almost never happens during a real sector panic.
